findings.evidence: `rg` shows `private def StronglyContinuousSemigroup.genQuot` at line 348, `def StronglyContinuousSemigroup.IsInGeneratorDomain ... := ∃ Ax, Filter.Tendsto (S.genQuot x) ...` at lines 355-357, `@[simp] theorem StronglyContinuousSemigroup.mem_domain_iff ... ↔ S.IsInGeneratorDomain x` at lines 425-427, while the usable public statement is only `mem_domain_iff_tendsto` at lines 437-441.
findings.fix: Make `IsInGeneratorDomain` private/internal or stop using it as the public normal form; expose the accessible quotient statement as the characteristic domain lemma, and do not have simp rewrite into a wrapper around a private name.
findings.issue: `@[simp] mem_domain_iff` rewrites `x ∈ S.domain` to the public wrapper `S.IsInGeneratorDomain x`, whose body is defined using the private helper `S.genQuot`; this makes the simp-normal public API less usable than the explicit tendsto characterization.

summary: The main generator and resolvent API is mostly present, but the domain normal form still exposes an implementation predicate tied to a private quotient helper. Public users should get the usable tendsto characterization directly, not an extra wrapper around inaccessible internals.
